title: Requests, Limits, QoS, Probes, Startup, Readiness, and Resource Failure · Independent Platform Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar namespace, failure domain, and policy constraint and must transfer requests, limits, qos, probes, startup, readiness, and resource failure into a new health and resource behavior policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of health and resource behavior policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate CPU and memory requests to scheduling and accounting, limits to runtime enforcement, and application concurrency to measured capacity.
Derive Guaranteed, Burstable, and BestEffort QoS, container OOM, Pod eviction, node pressure, and restart evidence from effective resources.
Separate startup gating, traffic readiness, process liveness, container restart, endpoint conditions, and dependency health.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Requests Limits Scheduling Runtime
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab service passes liveness while serving stale data, has no startup probe, requests 100m but uses 500m CPU, and is OOMKilled under pressure. Redesign probes, requests, limits, QoS, graceful termination, and changed-load checks.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Requests Limits Scheduling Runtime.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate cpu and memory requests to scheduling and accounting, limits to runtime enforcement, and application concurrency to measured capacity. For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Requests Limits Scheduling Runtime, keep K8s Requests Limits Scheduling Runtime connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-resource-probe-fault-tests-go3i55". Tailor its health-resource-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests, keep K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-resource-probe-fault-tests-go3i55
  - competency: k8s-resource-probe-fault-tests
    artifact: health-resource-policy.yaml
    decision: resource probe fault tests
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: go3i55
    change: |
      requests: {cpu: 200m, memory: 256Mi}
      limits: {memory: 512Mi}
      startupProbe: bounded-cold-start
      readinessProbe: traffic-contract
      livenessProbe: process-deadlock-only
      fault-test: throttling-and-oom
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl top pod status-api --containers
    failure: Reject dependency-based liveness, requests as runtime caps, green idle probes as capacity proof, or unexplained OOM and eviction.
    repair: Measure workload demand, separate probe purposes, calculate QoS, inject startup and pressure faults, and correlate container and node evidence.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests structural check is only one gate. health and resource behavior policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
